global.title: Controversial Ferrari Luce EV is an instant sellout in China
global.summary: All the controversy surrounding the launch of the first all-electric sedan to ever wear the Ferrari badge hasn’t slowed down sales any – every single Ferrari Luce allocated to the Chinese market is already spoken for.

global.title: Zero emission truck deployments SURGED up 37% in 2025 as momentum builds
global.summary: CALSTART’s Zeroing in on Zero-Emission Trucks report is out, and the results are in: after getting off to a slow start in the first half of 2025, zero-emission truck deployment in the US picked up steam in the second half, climbing to 4.14% of all new deployments.

global.title: Survey Sunday: with nearly a trillion dollars, will Elon try to make things right?
global.summary: Last week, we asked Electrek readers a simple whether they believed that Tesla and SpaceX CEO Elon Musk, after becoming the world’s first trillionaire, would finally make good on the ambitious promises he spent years making about accelerating sustainable energy, delivering unsupervised full self dri

global.title: Popular electric dirt bike Stark Varg lets owners download a new trick
global.summary: The Stark Varg has quickly become one of the most capable electric off-road motorcycles on the market (plus a pretty impressive on-road version as well), and now it’s getting another major performance upgrade without owners ever needing to visit a dealership.

global.title: Mercedes posts Tesla-like efficiency hauling 36 tons through a German winter
global.summary: Despite the rapidly growing number of heavy-duty EVs on western roads, a number of myths still persist about range, charging infrastructure, and economic viability. To combat those myths, Mercedes-Benz is sharing real-world data from 80 of its deployed electric semi trucks. Here’s what they found.

global.title: Tesla (TSLA) Q2 2026 delivery consensus: 406,000 vehicles expected
global.summary: Tesla released its company-compiled delivery consensus for the second quarter of 2026, and Wall Street analysts now expect the automaker to deliver 406,024 vehicles. That would be just 5.7% growth over the 384,122 vehicles Tesla delivered in Q2 2025 — a modest recovery for a company that has posted

global.title: The Jeep Recon EV goes on sale for $67,000 with a limited range
global.summary: Jeep’s electric off-roader is now on sale, starting at $67,000, but its driving range comes up a bit short of expectations.

global.title: BYD’s pickup spotted in China with a different look
global.summary: BYD’s plug-in hybrid pickup is already a hit in several overseas markets, but a new prototype was spotted driving in China with a slightly different design.

global.title: Podcast: Slate’s $25,000 electric pickup, Polestar banned from US, Tesla FSD crash, and more
global.summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Slate’s $25,000 electric pickup, Polestar being banned from the US, Tesla FSD crashes coming into focus, and more.

global.title: Slate’s electric truck can still dip under $20,000 — but only for some
global.summary: Slate Auto’s electric truck now starts at $24,950, but some buyers will still drive one home for under $20,000 thanks to state-level EV rebates. The catch is that those discounts depend heavily on where you live and how much you earn — and several of them may not apply to Slate at all.
